title: Welcome to Ohio State!

title: Welcome to Ohio State!
body: The Oval and Thompson library are my favorite places at OSU. I am so happy to be headed back as I just got accepted for grad school.
author: @RaabiaK2

title: Welcome to Ohio State!
body: I have a tip for any undergrad at Ohio State. When I went to OSU, the university kept old midterms and final exams in the department libraries because fraternities and sororities kept old midterms on file.  So, if you are taking math chemistry and Spanish, go the math, chemistry and Spanish libraries and ask if they have any old midterms for your class. I can't tell you how many times I found exams that were the same, or almost the same on file.
author: @TerrySaiter

title: PENN STATE PROS AND CONS #shorts #college #review #collegelife #school #schoollife

author: @Plut0n1um_

title: PENN STATE PROS AND CONS #shorts #college #review #collegelife #school #schoollife
body:
As someone who lived in the area as a non-student, there is lots of great restaurants too! And a lot of them are only open when school is in session.

Con - State College, the town were Penn State is located can be quite stressful to navigate sometimes, especially when school is in. The streets are quite narrow and there can be A LOT of vehicular traffic. Also, don't be surprised if people walk out in front of you in the streets while you're trying drive. Evennid you're the one that has the right-of-way. It happens A LOT.

When students are in, it almost has a city feel to it. At least downtown does.
author: @TMIATC

title: PENN STATE PROS AND CONS #shorts #college #review #collegelife #school #schoollife

title: PENN STATE PROS AND CONS #shorts #college #review #collegelife #school #schoollife
body: The weather is so all over the place here too. In Georgia, we complain about it all the time, but it's on steroids here. Sunny in the morning, looks like it's gonna storm 2 hours later, you think summer's finally here in April, and then the next week (after you've put away your winter stuff) it wants to go back to 50 degree days and 30 degree nights. ridiculous.

title: Top 10 things to know about OHIO STATE UNIVERSITY
body: The first thing to know is that to properly represent you always refer to it as THE OHIO STATE UNIVERSITY. The "THE" shows you're a part of the club. Sloopy's is the name of the restaurant/bar in the Student Union. This was all over the place. Anyhoo... I graduated in 2003 and I had an amazing experience. Good luck.

title: Is The Ohio State University the new University of Michigan?
body:
CM, the OSU out of state acceptance rate is @ 58%. The 50th percentile SAT score ranges between 1,280 and 1,430. Of I have a 1,500 SAT, is the acceptance rate for me higher than 58? Likewise, if my SAT is 1,200, is my acceptance rate higher than 58?

Thanks for your consideration.

title: Is The Ohio State University the new University of Michigan?

title: Is The Ohio State University the new University of Michigan?
body: Test optional at U-M is only for certain demographics. Asian and white students still need to submit SAT/ACT to have any chance, especially if they come from out of state. The whole test optional thing doesn’t apply to these students and they all know it.

title: Ohio University Campus Tour 💚
body: Historic passing. As of July 2022, the dormitory known as Scott Quadrangle, ( Scott Quad, in shorthand) is being demolished. It was very old, built at the beginning of the 1950s. It was unique because there aren't any other quadrangle buildings on OU's campus. In 1972 when I arrived at OU, it was old but serviceable, and it was a fun dorm to live in. In the 70s dorms hadn't embraced co-ed living yet. None of the dorms were set up that way, yet. In 1972 it was a males only dorm. There was nifty courtyard, that all the suites faced. It was a place to hang out in and party in, & smoke cigarettes. Back then almost none of the dorms had central air conditioning, students relied on window fans & open windows with cross-ventilation. The courtyard was a lively place as the students all had their windows open, so that their stereos could be heard outside.
author: @Davett53

title: Ohio University Campus Tour 💚
body: Class of 1976!..........Heady times,..1972 to 1976,.......The War in Vietnam was still raging, and the turbulent anti-war movement was in constant motion,  the counter culture scene was in full swing. Recreational drugs were everywhere. Athens was in a time warp and it felt like it was still in the 60s, some places just blocks away, it was still the late 1950s. Old cars were abandoned in the weeds on the other side of the river. Slow moving trains circumvented the town, cutting off people and cars from easy movement. The only food people ate was pizza,....there weren't too many alternatives. Hippies were buying up cheap land in surrounding areas and setting up communes. Dorm rooms were transformed into dens of psychedelic experimentation, with their walls decked out in black-light reactive posters. The clap-trap of "head shops", included strobe lights, portable light shows & reflective beads, fake crystals, that dangled down beneath ceilings covered in surplus parachutes.   There was an explosion of new ways to listen to music, the stereo revolution had begun. Drinking to wretched excess was well established, before my class year arrived. We piled on the drugs, from the mildly benign, to the insanely life changing. Yet somehow I survived and thrived. I found my niche, my people and my passion. No one was more surprised than myself, when I won a 3 year art scholarship, based on portfolios of new art I created and submitted. And equally uncanny, that I graduated with the honors, cum laude. Who would have believed that Athens which was in the middle of no where, was also simultaneously the nexus of everything.
author: @Davett53
